content.problemStatement:
There were initially XXX million people in a town, out of which YYY million people left the town and ZZZ million people immigrated to this town.

Determine the final population of town in millions.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case consists of three integers XXX, YYY and ZZZ.


Output Format
For each test case, output the final population of the town in millions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The initial population of the town was 333 million, out of which 111 million people left and 222 million people entered the town. So, final population =3−1+2=4= 3 - 1 + 2 = 4=3−1+2=4 million.
Test case 222: The initial population of the town was 222 million, out of which 222 million left and 222 million immigrated. The final population is thus 2+2−2=22+2-2 = 22+2−2=2 million.
